title: glandularia lambertii
intro: Glandularia lambertii, also known as Rosita, is a type of flowering plant in the verbena family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Glandularia lambertii has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to promote healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide a broader range of health benefits.
